body.title: ProviderHealthClassifier — classify ping failures before fallthrough
body.result: ProviderHealthClassifier: unified exception classification for ping failures (local_bug, auth_error, unreachable, unknown)
body.intention: I intend that ping failures are categorized by exception type and logged before fallthrough, so we can immediately distinguish our bugs from provider outages.
body.description:
When a source ping fails, ToolLoop/HealthMonitor currently logs only 'unavailable — falling through' before routing to a more expensive source. This masks whether the failure is our bug (DNS, auth) or a real outage and caused at least one mis-route from flat-rate Ollama to paid OR on 2026-06-11.

Add devices/inference/provider_health.py with ProviderHealthClassifier class:
  classify(source_name, failure_exc) -> str
  Returns: local_bug (socket.gaierror/DNS, connection refused), auth_error (HTTPError 401/403), unreachable (timeout, other connection errors), unknown (anything else)

Add Source._classify_ping_failure(exc) -> str to the Source base class in sources.py. Each subclass ping() except block calls self._classify_ping_failure(exc) before returning False. This is the ONLY correct injection point — ping() swallows exceptions before they reach HealthMonitor, so the classifier must live inside the except blocks.

Log at INFO inside _classify_ping_failure:
  log.info('source %s unavailable: failure_category=%s', self.name, category)

Source.ping() return type stays bool. Status-page fetch is explicitly NOT in scope (separate background enrichment if ever needed). provider_health.py is a helper module local to devices/inference/ — not part of the Classifier rack device (D-classifier-device-architecture-2026-06-12).

**Affected files:** new devices/inference/provider_health.py (LOW inertia); devices/inference/sources.py — Source base class + subclass except blocks (MEDIUM inertia)
**Design rules:** OOP-first (ProviderHealthClassifier class, not standalone fns); log every interface crossing; urllib-only (no requests import)
**Scope boundary:** In: exception-type classifier + log line in ping() failure path. Out: status-page fetch, changing Source.ping() return type, changing fallthrough routing logic.
**Completion criteria:** pytest tests/inference/test_provider_health.py passes; grep 'failure_category=' in source logs fires on next DS run against currently-failing source.
**Observable via:** log.info('source %s unavailable: failure_category=%s', source_name, category) in Source._classify_ping_failure().
**Validation:** after any ping() failure, datacenter_logs/inference/ contains 'failure_category=' line. Acceptable via next DS run against failing provider OR targeted test with mock-unreachable source.
**Cleanup:** pytest fixture sets caplog level to WARNING for devices.inference.sources so INFO classification lines are suppressed; no DB rows or channel posts generated.
